11: ---
12: 
13: # JavaScript Code Quality Checklist
14: 
15: Quality validation checklist for JavaScript code in the OpenCode development environment.
16: 
17: ---
18: 
19: ## 1. OVERVIEW
20: 
21: ### Purpose
22: 
23: Specific quality checks for JavaScript files. Use alongside the [universal_checklist.md](./universal_checklist.md) for complete validation.
24: 
25: ### Priority Levels
26: 
27: | Level | Meaning | Enforcement |
28: |-------|---------|-------------|
29: | P0 | HARD BLOCKER | Must fix before commit |
30: | P1 | Required | Must fix or get explicit approval |
31: | P2 | Recommended | Can defer with justification |
32: 
33: ---
34: 
35: ## 2. P0 - HARD BLOCKERS
36: 
37: These items MUST be fixed before any commit.
38: 
39: ### Box Header
40: 
41: ```markdown
42: [ ] File has box header with component identification
43: ```
44: 
45: **Required format**:
46: ```javascript
47: // ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
48: // ║ COMPONENT: [Component Name]                                               ║
49: // ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
50: // ║ PURPOSE: [Brief description of what this file does]                       ║
51: // ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
52: ```
53: 
54: ### 'use strict'
55: 
56: ```markdown
57: [ ] 'use strict' directive present at top of file
58: ```
59: 
60: **Required** for `.js/.cjs` files (immediately after header):
61: ```javascript
62: 'use strict';
63: ```
64: 
65: **Exception**: `.mjs` files and OpenCode plugin ESM paths are strict by module
66: semantics; the verifier intentionally skips `.mjs` strict-mode enforcement.
67: 
68: ### Function Naming
69: 
70: ```markdown
71: [ ] All functions use camelCase naming
72: ```
73: 
74: **Correct**:
75: ```javascript
76: function validateInput(data) { }
77: function processFile(path) { }
78: const handleError = (err) => { };
79: ```
80: 
81: **Wrong**:
82: ```javascript
83: function validate_input(data) { }    // snake_case
84: function ProcessFile(path) { }       // PascalCase
85: ```
86: 
87: ### No Commented-Out Code
88: 
89: ```markdown
90: [ ] No commented-out code blocks
91: ```
92: 
93: **Remove**:
94: ```javascript
95: // function old_implementation() {
96: //     return "deprecated";
97: // }
98: ```
99: 
100: ### WHY Comments
101: 
102: ```markdown
103: [ ] Purposeful WHY comments only (max 3 comments per 10 LOC)
104:     - Comments explain WHY, not WHAT
105:     - No narrative comments describing obvious mechanics
106: ```
107: 
108: **Required for**:
109: - Algorithm choices
110: - Performance optimizations
111: - Business rule implementations
112: - Workarounds for known issues
113: 
114: ### Numbered ALL-CAPS Sections
115: 
116: ```markdown
117: [ ] Numbered ALL-CAPS section headers are preserved (`## N. SECTION NAME`)
118: ```
119: 
120: ---
121: 
122: ## 3. P1 - REQUIRED
123: 
124: These must be addressed or receive approval to defer.
125: 
126: ### Module Exports by Path
127: 
128: ```markdown
129: [ ] Module export style matches the file path and package boundary
130: ```
131: 
132: **Correct for non-plugin `.js/.cjs` utilities**:
133: ```javascript
134: module.exports = { functionName, CONSTANT };
135: module.exports = functionName;
136: ```
137: 
138: **Correct for OpenCode plugin loader paths**:
139: ```javascript
140: export default async function ExamplePlugin(ctx, options = {}) {
141:   return {};
142: }
143: ```
144: 
145: **Plugin paths**: Files in `.opencode/plugins/` and
146: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/` MUST use ESM
147: (`export default`) — see `quality_standards.md` §10 OpenCode Plugin Exemption
148: Tier.
149: 
150: **Wrong for non-plugin CommonJS utilities**:
151: ```javascript
152: export { function_name };
153: export default function_name;
154: ```
155: 
156: ### Guard Clauses
157: 
158: ```markdown
159: [ ] Functions use early returns for invalid states
160: ```
161: 
162: **Correct**:
163: ```javascript
164: function processData(data) {
165:     if (!data) return null;
166:     if (!data.requiredField) return null;
167: 
168:     // Main logic here
169:     return result;
170: }
171: ```
172: 
173: **Wrong**:
174: ```javascript
175: function processData(data) {
176:     if (data) {
177:         if (data.requiredField) {
178:             // Deeply nested logic
179:         }
180:     }
181: }
182: ```
183: 
184: ### Bracketed Logging
185: 
186: ```markdown
187: [ ] Console output uses bracketed format
188: ```
189: 
190: **Correct**:
191: ```javascript
192: console.log('[COMPONENT] Processing file:', filename);
193: console.error('[COMPONENT] Error:', error.message);
194: ```
195: 
196: **Wrong**:
197: ```javascript
198: console.log('Processing file: ' + filename);
199: console.log(filename);  // No context
200: ```
201: 
202: ### Constants
203: 
204: ```markdown
205: [ ] Module-level constants use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE
206: ```
207: 
208: **Correct**:
209: ```javascript
210: const MAX_RETRIES = 3;
211: const DEFAULT_TIMEOUT = 5000;
212: const VALID_TYPES = ['a', 'b', 'c'];
213: ```
214: 
215: ### Error Handling
216: 
217: ```markdown
218: [ ] Try/catch blocks handle errors appropriately
219: [ ] Error messages include context
220: ```
221: 
222: **Correct**:
223: ```javascript
224: try {
225:     const data = JSON.parse(content);
226: } catch (error) {
227:     console.error('[PARSER] Failed to parse JSON:', error.message);
228:     throw new Error(`Invalid JSON in ${filename}: ${error.message}`);
229: }
230: ```
231: 
232: ### KISS / DRY / SOLID Checks
233: 
234: ```markdown
235: [ ] KISS: avoid unnecessary abstraction or layering
236: [ ] DRY: repeated rules/constants extracted to shared source
237: [ ] SOLID: SRP/OCP/LSP/ISP/DIP violations checked and documented
238: ```
239: 
240: ---
241: 
242: ## 4. P2 - RECOMMENDED
243: 
244: These improve quality but can be deferred.
245: 
246: ### JSDoc Comments
247: 
248: ```markdown
249: [ ] Public functions have JSDoc documentation
250: ```
251: 
252: **Example**:
253: ```javascript
254: /**
255:  * Validates input data against schema.
256:  * @param {Object} data - The data to validate
257:  * @param {Object} schema - Validation schema
258:  * @returns {Object} Validation result with isValid and errors
259:  */
260: function validateInput(data, schema) { }
261: ```
262: 
263: ### Consistent String Quotes
264: 
265: ```markdown
266: [ ] Strings use single quotes consistently
267: ```
268: 
269: **Preferred**:
270: ```javascript
271: const message = 'Hello world';
272: const config = { key: 'value' };
273: ```
274: 
275: ### Async/Await Pattern
276: 
277: ```markdown
278: [ ] Async operations use async/await (not callbacks where possible)
279: ```
280: 
281: **Preferred**:
282: ```javascript
283: async function fetchData() {
284:     const response = await fetch(url);
285:     const data = await response.json();
286:     return data;
287: }
288: ```
289: 
290: ### Destructuring
291: 
292: ```markdown
293: [ ] Object destructuring used where it improves clarity
294: ```
295: 
296: **Good**:
297: ```javascript
298: const { name, value } = config;
299: function process({ data, options = {} }) { }
300: ```
301: 
302: ---
303: 
304: ## 5. CHECKLIST TEMPLATE
305: 
306: For formal findings-first review output, use `sk-code-review` as the baseline and treat this file as JavaScript surface evidence.
307: 
308: ```markdown
309: ## JavaScript Standards Surface Evidence
310: 
311: - [ ] JavaScript standards validated in `javascript_checklist.md`
312: - [ ] Universal standards validated in `universal_checklist.md`
313: - [ ] Findings severity/order produced with `sk-code-review/references/quick_reference.md`
314: - [ ] Baseline security, quality, and test checks sourced from `sk-code-review`
315: - [ ] Surface-specific deviations documented with file:line evidence
316: ```
317: 
318: ---
319: 
320: ## 6. VALIDATION COMMANDS
321: 
322: ```bash
323: # Syntax check
324: node --check file.js
325: 
326: # ESLint (if configured)
327: npx eslint file.js
328: 
329: # Check for common issues in non-plugin CommonJS utilities
330: grep -n "function [a-z]*_[a-z]" file.js  # Find snake_case functions
331: grep -n "^export " file.js              # Review: allowed only for ESM/plugin paths
332: ```
333: 
334: ---
335: 
336: ## 7. RELATED RESOURCES
337: 
338: ### Checklists

341: ### Style Guide

---
title: JavaScript Code Quality Checklist
description: Quality validation checklist for JavaScript code in the OpenCode development environment.
trigger_phrases:
  - "opencode javascript checklist"

# JavaScript Code Quality Checklist

Quality validation checklist for JavaScript code in the OpenCode development environment.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

### Purpose

Specific quality checks for JavaScript files. Use alongside the [universal_checklist.md](./universal_checklist.md) for complete validation.

### Priority Levels

| Level | Meaning | Enforcement |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| P0 | HARD BLOCKER | Must fix before commit |
| P1 | Required | Must fix or get explicit approval |
| P2 | Recommended | Can defer with justification |

---

## 2. P0 - HARD BLOCKERS

These items MUST be fixed before any commit.

### Box Header

```markdown
[ ] File has box header with component identification
```

**Required format**:
```javascript
// ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
// ║ COMPONENT: [Component Name]                                               ║
// ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
// ║ PURPOSE: [Brief description of what this file does]                       ║
// ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

### 'use strict'

```markdown
[ ] 'use strict' directive present at top of file
```

**Required** for `.js/.cjs` files (immediately after header):
```javascript
'use strict';
```

**Exception**: `.mjs` files and OpenCode plugin ESM paths are strict by module
semantics; the verifier intentionally skips `.mjs` strict-mode enforcement.

### Function Naming

```markdown
[ ] All functions use camelCase naming
```

**Correct**:
```javascript
function validateInput(data) { }
function processFile(path) { }
const handleError = (err) => { };
```

**Wrong**:
```javascript
function validate_input(data) { }    // snake_case
function ProcessFile(path) { }       // PascalCase
```

### No Commented-Out Code

```markdown
[ ] No commented-out code blocks
```

**Remove**:
```javascript
// function old_implementation() {
//     return "deprecated";
// }
```

### WHY Comments

```markdown
[ ] Purposeful WHY comments only (max 3 comments per 10 LOC)
    - Comments explain WHY, not WHAT
    - No narrative comments describing obvious mechanics
```

**Required for**:
- Algorithm choices
- Performance optimizations
- Business rule implementations
- Workarounds for known issues

### Numbered ALL-CAPS Sections

```markdown
[ ] Numbered ALL-CAPS section headers are preserved (`## N. SECTION NAME`)
```

---

## 3. P1 - REQUIRED

These must be addressed or receive approval to defer.

### Module Exports by Path

```markdown
[ ] Module export style matches the file path and package boundary
```

**Correct for non-plugin `.js/.cjs` utilities**:
```javascript
module.exports = { functionName, CONSTANT };
module.exports = functionName;
```

**Correct for OpenCode plugin loader paths**:
```javascript
export default async function ExamplePlugin(ctx, options = {}) {
  return {};
}
```

**Plugin paths**: Files in `.opencode/plugins/` and
`.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/` MUST use ESM
(`export default`) — see `quality_standards.md` §10 OpenCode Plugin Exemption
Tier.

**Wrong for non-plugin CommonJS utilities**:
```javascript
export { function_name };
export default function_name;
```

### Guard Clauses

```markdown
[ ] Functions use early returns for invalid states
```

**Correct**:
```javascript
function processData(data) {
    if (!data) return null;
    if (!data.requiredField) return null;

    // Main logic here
    return result;
}
```

**Wrong**:
```javascript
function processData(data) {
    if (data) {
        if (data.requiredField) {
            // Deeply nested logic
        }
    }
}
```

### Bracketed Logging

```markdown
[ ] Console output uses bracketed format
```

**Correct**:
```javascript
console.log('[COMPONENT] Processing file:', filename);
console.error('[COMPONENT] Error:', error.message);
```

**Wrong**:
```javascript
console.log('Processing file: ' + filename);
console.log(filename);  // No context
```

### Constants

```markdown
[ ] Module-level constants use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE
```

**Correct**:
```javascript
const MAX_RETRIES = 3;
const DEFAULT_TIMEOUT = 5000;
const VALID_TYPES = ['a', 'b', 'c'];
```

### Error Handling

```markdown
[ ] Try/catch blocks handle errors appropriately
[ ] Error messages include context
```

**Correct**:
```javascript
try {
    const data = JSON.parse(content);
} catch (error) {
    console.error('[PARSER] Failed to parse JSON:', error.message);
    throw new Error(`Invalid JSON in ${filename}: ${error.message}`);
}
```

### KISS / DRY / SOLID Checks

```markdown
[ ] KISS: avoid unnecessary abstraction or layering
[ ] DRY: repeated rules/constants extracted to shared source
[ ] SOLID: SRP/OCP/LSP/ISP/DIP violations checked and documented
```

---

## 4. P2 - RECOMMENDED

These improve quality but can be deferred.

### JSDoc Comments

```markdown
[ ] Public functions have JSDoc documentation
```

**Example**:
```javascript
/**
 * Validates input data against schema.
 * @param {Object} data - The data to validate
 * @param {Object} schema - Validation schema
 * @returns {Object} Validation result with isValid and errors
 */
function validateInput(data, schema) { }
```

### Consistent String Quotes

```markdown
[ ] Strings use single quotes consistently
```

**Preferred**:
```javascript
const message = 'Hello world';
const config = { key: 'value' };
```

### Async/Await Pattern

```markdown
[ ] Async operations use async/await (not callbacks where possible)
```

**Preferred**:
```javascript
async function fetchData() {
    const response = await fetch(url);
    const data = await response.json();
    return data;
}
```

### Destructuring

```markdown
[ ] Object destructuring used where it improves clarity
```

**Good**:
```javascript
const { name, value } = config;
function process({ data, options = {} }) { }
```

---

## 5. CHECKLIST TEMPLATE

For formal findings-first review output, use `sk-code-review` as the baseline and treat this file as JavaScript surface evidence.

```markdown
## JavaScript Standards Surface Evidence

- [ ] JavaScript standards validated in `javascript_checklist.md`
- [ ] Universal standards validated in `universal_checklist.md`
- [ ] Findings severity/order produced with `sk-code-review/references/quick_reference.md`
- [ ] Baseline security, quality, and test checks sourced from `sk-code-review`
- [ ] Surface-specific deviations documented with file:line evidence
```

---

## 6. VALIDATION COMMANDS

```bash
# Syntax check
node --check file.js

# ESLint (if configured)
npx eslint file.js

# Check for common issues in non-plugin CommonJS utilities
grep -n "function [a-z]*_[a-z]" file.js  # Find snake_case functions
grep -n "^export " file.js              # Review: allowed only for ESM/plugin paths
```

---

## 7. RELATED RESOURCES

### Checklists
- [universal_checklist.md](./universal_checklist.md) - Language-agnostic checks

### Style Guide
- [JavaScript Style Guide](../../../references/opencode/javascript/style_guide.md)
- [JavaScript Quality Standards](../../../references/opencode/javascript/quality_standards.md)

1: // ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
2: // ║ mk-code-graph OpenCode Plugin                                            ║
3: // ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
4: 'use strict';
5: 
6: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
7: // 1. OVERVIEW
8: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
9: // Thin OpenCode plugin hook layer.
10: // Important runtime boundary: never import the MCP server bundle directly
11: // inside the OpenCode host process because its native modules (better-sqlite3,
12: // sqlite-vec) may be compiled for a different Node ABI than the host runtime.
13: // Instead, call a plain `node` bridge process that loads the server bundle in
14: // the user's normal Node environment and returns the transport plan as JSON.
15: 
16: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
17: // 2. IMPORTS
18: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
19: 
20: import { randomUUID } from 'node:crypto';
21: import { execFile } from 'node:child_process';
22: import { readFile } from 'node:fs/promises';
23: import { homedir } from 'node:os';
24: import { join } from 'node:path';
25: import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';

27: import { tool } from '@opencode-ai/plugin/tool';

32:   isMessageAnchorLike,
33: } from '../skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/spec-kit-opencode-message-schema.mjs';
34: 
35: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
36: // 3. CONSTANTS AND TYPES
37: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
38: 
39: const PLUGIN_ID = 'mk-code-graph';
40: const DEFAULT_CACHE_TTL_MS = 5000;
41: const DEFAULT_BRIDGE_TIMEOUT_MS = 15000;
42: const DEFAULT_NODE_BINARY = 'node';
43: const RESUME_MODE = 'minimal';
44: const MESSAGES_TRANSFORM_ENABLED = true;
45: const MESSAGES_TRANSFORM_MODE = 'schema_aligned';
46: const SYNTHETIC_METADATA_KEY = 'mkCodeGraph';
47: const BRIDGE_PATH = fileURLToPath(new URL('../skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/mk-code-graph-bridge.mjs', import.meta.url));
48: 
49: async function loadConfig() {
50:   const path = join(homedir(), '.config', 'opencode', 'plugin', 'mk-code-graph.json');
51:   try {
52:     const raw = await readFile(path, 'utf-8');
53:     return JSON.parse(raw);
54:   } catch {
55:     return {};
56:   }
57: }
58: 
59: const configPromise = loadConfig();
60: 
61: /**
62:  * @typedef {{
63:  *   cacheTtlMs?: number,
64:  *   specFolder?: string,
65:  *   nodeBinary?: string,
66:  *   bridgeTimeoutMs?: number,
67:  * }} PluginOptions
68:  */
69: 
70: /**
71:  * @typedef {{
72:  *   hook: string,
73:  *   title: string,
74:  *   payloadKind: string,
75:  *   dedupeKey: string,
76:  *   content: string,
77:  * }} TransportBlock
78:  */
79: 
80: /**
81:  * @typedef {{
82:  *   interfaceVersion: string,
83:  *   transportOnly: true,
84:  *   retrievalPolicyOwner: string,
85:  *   event: {
86:  *     hook: string,
87:  *     trackedPayloadKinds: string[],
88:  *     summary: string,
89:  *   },
90:  *   systemTransform?: TransportBlock,
91:  *   messagesTransform: TransportBlock[],
92:  *   compaction?: TransportBlock,
93:  * }} TransportPlan
94:  */
95: 
96: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
97: // 4. MODULE STATE
98: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
99: 
100: const transportCache = new Map();
101: let runtimeReady = false;
102: let lastRuntimeError = null;
103: 
104: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
105: // 5. HELPERS
106: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
107: 
108: function normalizePositiveInt(value, fallback) {
109:   return Number.isFinite(value) && value > 0 ? Math.trunc(value) : fallback;
110: }
111: 
112: function stableStringify(value) {
113:   if (!value || typeof value !== 'object') {
114:     return JSON.stringify(String(value));
115:   }
116: 
117:   if (Array.isArray(value)) {
118:     return `[${value.map((entry) => stableStringify(entry)).join(',')}]`;
119:   }
120: 
121:   return `{${Object.keys(value)
122:     .sort()
123:     .map((key) => `${JSON.stringify(key)}:${stableStringify(value[key])}`)
124:     .join(',')}}`;
125: }
126: 
127: function normalizeSessionID(value) {
128:   if (value === null || value === undefined || value === '') {
129:     return '__global__';
130:   }
131:   if (typeof value === 'object') {
132:     return stableStringify(value);
133:   }
134:   return String(value);
135: }
136: 
137: function normalizeOptions(rawOptions) {
138:   const envCacheTTLMs = Number(process.env.MK_CODE_GRAPH_CACHE_TTL_MS);
139:   const envNodeBinary = process.env.MK_CODE_GRAPH_NODE_BINARY;
140:   const envBridgeTimeoutMs = Number(process.env.MK_CODE_GRAPH_BRIDGE_TIMEOUT_MS);
141: 
142:   if (!rawOptions || typeof rawOptions !== 'object') {
143:     return {
144:       cacheTtlMs: normalizePositiveInt(envCacheTTLMs, DEFAULT_CACHE_TTL_MS),
145:       specFolder: process.env.MK_CODE_GRAPH_SPEC_FOLDER || undefined,
146:       nodeBinary: envNodeBinary || process.env.SPEC_KIT_PLUGIN_NODE_BINARY || DEFAULT_NODE_BINARY,
147:       bridgeTimeoutMs: normalizePositiveInt(envBridgeTimeoutMs, DEFAULT_BRIDGE_TIMEOUT_MS),
148:     };
149:   }
150: 
151:   const options = /** @type {PluginOptions} */ (rawOptions);
152:   return {
153:     cacheTtlMs: normalizePositiveInt(options.cacheTtlMs, normalizePositiveInt(envCacheTTLMs, DEFAULT_CACHE_TTL_MS)),
154:     specFolder: typeof options.specFolder === 'string' && options.specFolder.trim()
155:       ? options.specFolder.trim()
156:       : (process.env.MK_CODE_GRAPH_SPEC_FOLDER || undefined),
157:     nodeBinary: typeof options.nodeBinary === 'string' && options.nodeBinary.trim()
158:       ? options.nodeBinary.trim()
159:       : (envNodeBinary || process.env.SPEC_KIT_PLUGIN_NODE_BINARY || DEFAULT_NODE_BINARY),
160:     bridgeTimeoutMs: normalizePositiveInt(options.bridgeTimeoutMs, normalizePositiveInt(envBridgeTimeoutMs, DEFAULT_BRIDGE_TIMEOUT_MS)),
161:   };
162: }
163: 
164: function cacheKeyForSession(sessionID, specFolder) {
165:   return `${specFolder ?? '__workspace__'}::${normalizeSessionID(sessionID)}`;
166: }
167: 
168: export function parseTransportPlan(responseText) {
169:   if (typeof responseText !== 'string') {
170:     // OpenCode 1.3.17's legacy loader invokes named function exports as plugins.
171:     // Return an empty hook object rather than a null hook if it calls this parser.
172:     return {};
173:   }
174:   if (!responseText) {
175:     return null;
176:   }
177: 
178:   try {
179:     const parsed = JSON.parse(responseText);
180:     const data = parsed?.data ?? parsed;
181:     const plan = data?.opencodeTransport;
182:     if (!plan || typeof plan !== 'object') {
183:       return null;
184:     }
185:     if (plan.transportOnly !== true || !Array.isArray(plan.messagesTransform)) {
186:       return null;
187:     }
188:     return /** @type {TransportPlan} */ (plan);
189:   } catch {
190:     return null;
191:   }
192: }
193: 
194: function diagnoseTransportPlanFailure(responseText) {
195:   if (!responseText) {
196:     return 'Bridge returned empty stdout; plugin injection will no-op';
197:   }
198: 
199:   try {
200:     const parsed = JSON.parse(responseText);
201:     if (parsed?.status === 'skipped' || parsed?.status === 'fail_open') {
202:       const exitCode = parsed?.metadata?.exitCode ?? null;
203:       const reason = parsed?.error || parsed?.metadata?.reason || parsed?.metadata?.route || 'no transport payload';
204:       return `Bridge ${parsed.status}: ${reason}${exitCode === null || exitCode === undefined ? '' : ` (exit=${exitCode})`}; plugin injection will no-op`;
205:     }
206:     const data = parsed?.data ?? parsed;
207:     const plan = data?.opencodeTransport;
208:     if (!plan || typeof plan !== 'object') {
209:       return 'Bridge response missing data.opencodeTransport; plugin injection will no-op';
210:     }
211:     if (plan.transportOnly !== true) {
212:       return 'Bridge opencodeTransport.transportOnly was not true; plugin injection will no-op';
213:     }
214:     if (!Array.isArray(plan.messagesTransform)) {
215:       return 'Bridge opencodeTransport.messagesTransform was not an array; plugin injection will no-op';
216:     }
217:     return 'Bridge returned invalid OpenCode transport payload; plugin injection will no-op';
218:   } catch (error) {
219:     const reason = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
220:     return `Bridge returned unparsable JSON (${reason}); plugin injection will no-op`;
221:   }
222: }
223: 
224: // Raw stderr writes while the opencode TUI is active render into the user's
225: // input field, so a bridge-skip diagnostic must stay silent by default. The
226: // failure is non-fatal (injection no-ops) and remains inspectable as
227: // last_runtime_error via the plugin's status tool.
228: function emitRuntimeDiagnostic(message) {
229:   if (!process.env.MK_CODE_GRAPH_DEBUG) {
230:     return;
231:   }
232:   process.stderr.write(`[${PLUGIN_ID}] ${message}\n`);
233: }
234: 
235: function stringifyError(error) {
236:   if (!error) {
237:     return 'Unknown bridge error';
238:   }
239:   if (error instanceof Error) {
240:     return error.message;
241:   }
242:   return String(error);
243: }
244: 
245: function execFilePromise(file, args, options) {
246:   return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
247:     execFile(file, args, options, (error, stdout, stderr) => {
248:       if (error) {
249:         reject(error);
250:         return;
251:       }
252:       resolve({
253:         stdout: typeof stdout === 'string' ? stdout : '',
254:         stderr: typeof stderr === 'string' ? stderr : '',
255:       });
256:     });
257:   });
258: }
259: 
260: async function runTransportBridge({ projectDir, specFolder, nodeBinary, bridgeTimeoutMs }) {
261:   const args = [BRIDGE_PATH, '--minimal', '--timeout-ms', String(bridgeTimeoutMs)];
262:   if (specFolder) {
263:     args.push('--spec-folder', specFolder);
264:   }
265: 
266:   const result = await execFilePromise(nodeBinary, args, {
267:     cwd: projectDir,
268:     env: process.env,
269:     timeout: bridgeTimeoutMs,
270:     maxBuffer: 1024 * 1024,
271:   });
272:   const stdout = result.stdout.trim();
273:   const plan = parseTransportPlan(stdout);
274:   return {
275:     plan,
276:     diagnostic: plan ? null : diagnoseTransportPlanFailure(stdout),
277:   };
278: }
279: 
280: async function loadTransportPlan({ projectDir, sessionID, specFolder, cacheTtlMs, nodeBinary, bridgeTimeoutMs }) {
281:   const key = cacheKeyForSession(sessionID, specFolder);
282:   const now = Date.now();
283:   const cached = transportCache.get(key);
284: 
285:   if (cached && cached.expiresAt > now) {
286:     return cached.plan;
287:   }
288: 
289:   try {
290:     const bridgeResult = await runTransportBridge({
291:       projectDir,
292:       specFolder,
293:       nodeBinary,
294:       bridgeTimeoutMs,
295:     });
296:     const plan = bridgeResult.plan;
297: 
298:     if (!plan) {
299:       lastRuntimeError = bridgeResult.diagnostic || 'Bridge returned no OpenCode transport payload';
300:       emitRuntimeDiagnostic(lastRuntimeError);
301:       runtimeReady = false;
302:       transportCache.delete(key);
303:       return null;
304:     }
305: 
306:     runtimeReady = true;
307:     lastRuntimeError = null;
308:     transportCache.set(key, {
309:       plan,
310:       expiresAt: now + cacheTtlMs,
311:       updatedAt: new Date(now).toISOString(),
312:     });
313:     return plan;
314:   } catch (error) {
315:     runtimeReady = false;
316:     lastRuntimeError = stringifyError(error);
317:     transportCache.delete(key);
318:     return null;
319:   }
320: }
321: 
322: function extractEventSessionID(event) {
323:   if (!event || typeof event !== 'object') {
324:     return null;
325:   }
326: 
327:   if (typeof event.sessionID === 'string') {
328:     return event.sessionID;
329:   }
330:   if (event.sessionID && typeof event.sessionID === 'object') {
331:     return normalizeSessionID(event.sessionID);
332:   }
333: 
334:   if (event.properties && typeof event.properties === 'object') {
335:     if (typeof event.properties.sessionID === 'string') {
336:       return event.properties.sessionID;
337:     }
338:     if (event.properties.sessionID && typeof event.properties.sessionID === 'object') {
339:       return normalizeSessionID(event.properties.sessionID);
340:     }
341:     if (event.properties.info && typeof event.properties.info === 'object') {
342:       if (typeof event.properties.info.sessionID === 'string') {
343:         return event.properties.info.sessionID;
344:       }
345:       if (event.properties.info.sessionID && typeof event.properties.info.sessionID === 'object') {
346:         return normalizeSessionID(event.properties.info.sessionID);
347:       }
348:       if (typeof event.properties.info.id === 'string') {
349:         return event.properties.info.id;
350:       }
351:     }
352:     if (event.properties.part && typeof event.properties.part === 'object' && typeof event.properties.part.sessionID === 'string') {
353:       return event.properties.part.sessionID;
354:     }
355:     if (event.properties.part && typeof event.properties.part === 'object' && event.properties.part.sessionID && typeof event.properties.part.sessionID === 'object') {
356:       return normalizeSessionID(event.properties.part.sessionID);
357:     }
358:   }
359: 
360:   return null;
361: }
362: 
363: function shouldInvalidateEvent(eventType) {
364:   return typeof eventType === 'string'
365:     && (eventType.startsWith('session.') || eventType.startsWith('message.'));
366: }
367: 
368: function invalidateTransportCache(sessionID, specFolder) {
369:   if (sessionID) {
370:     transportCache.delete(cacheKeyForSession(sessionID, specFolder));
371:     return;
372:   }

1: #!/usr/bin/env node
2: /**
3:  * OpenCode Plugin: session MCP cleanup
4:  *
5:  * OpenCode has no JSON SessionEnd hook (unlike Claude's Stop hook), so the
6:  * dispose lifecycle event is its session-end equivalent.
7:  * On teardown this runs the runtime-agnostic session-cleanup.sh to reclaim the
8:  * session's MCP helper descendants — the OpenCode parity of the Claude Stop
9:  * cleanup wiring.
10:  *
11:  * Best-effort and bounded: it never blocks or fails teardown.
12:  *
13:  * Plugin signature follows opencode's plugin API: a default-exported async
14:  * factory that returns lifecycle hooks.
15:  */
16: 
17: const { spawnSync } = require("node:child_process");
18: const path = require("node:path");
19: 
20: const PLUGIN_ROOT = __dirname;
21: const REPO_ROOT = path.resolve(PLUGIN_ROOT, "..", "..");
22: 
23: export default async function sessionCleanupPlugin() {
24:   return {
25:     // The host invokes this for every lifecycle event.
26:     async event({ event }) {
27:       // Some opencode builds wrap the payload as { event }, others pass it raw.
28:       const input = arguments?.[0] ?? {};
29:       const eventType = event?.type ?? input?.event?.type;
30: 
31:       if (eventType === 'server.instance.disposed' || eventType === 'global.disposed') {
32:         try {
33:           spawnSync('bash', [path.join(REPO_ROOT, '.opencode/scripts/session-cleanup.sh')], {
34:             cwd: REPO_ROOT,
35:             timeout: 8000,
36:           });
37:         } catch (_) {
38:           // best-effort; never block teardown
39:         }
40:       }
41:     },
42:   };
43: }

/**
 * OpenCode Plugin: session MCP cleanup
 *
 * OpenCode has no JSON SessionEnd hook (unlike Claude's Stop hook), so the
 * dispose lifecycle event is its session-end equivalent.
 * On teardown this runs the runtime-agnostic session-cleanup.sh to reclaim the
 * session's MCP helper descendants — the OpenCode parity of the Claude Stop
 * cleanup wiring.
 *
 * Best-effort and bounded: it never blocks or fails teardown.
 *
 * Plugin signature follows opencode's plugin API: a default-exported async
 * factory that returns lifecycle hooks.
 */

const path = require("node:path");

const PLUGIN_ROOT = __dirname;

373:   return {
374:     event: async ({ event }) => {
375:       const eventType = eventTypeFrom(event);
376:       if (eventType === 'session.created') {
377:         if (options.enabled) state.runtimeReady = true;
378:         return;
379:       }
380:       if (eventType === 'session.deleted') {
381:         invalidateSession(extractEventSessionID(event));
382:         return;
383:       }
384:       if (typeof eventType === 'string' && (eventType.startsWith('message.') || eventType.startsWith('session.'))) {
385:         invalidateSession(extractEventSessionID(event));
386:         return;
387:       }
388:       if (eventType === 'server.instance.disposed' || eventType === 'global.disposed') {
389:         resetRuntimeState();
390:       }
391:     },
392: 
393:     'experimental.chat.system.transform': appendContinuityBrief,
394: 
395:     tool: {
396:       mk_spec_memory_status: tool({
397:         description: 'Show Spec Kit Memory OpenCode plugin and warm CLI bridge status',
398:         args: {},
399:         async execute() {
400:           const bridgeStatus = options.enabled
401:             ? await runBridge({ request: 'status', sessionID: '__global__' })
402:             : { status: 'skipped', metadata: {}, error: state.disabledReason ?? 'disabled' };
403:           return [
404:             `plugin_id=${PLUGIN_ID}`,
405:             `enabled=${options.enabled}`,
406:             `disabled_reason=${state.disabledReason ?? 'none'}`,
407:             `cache_ttl_ms=${options.cacheTtlMs}`,
408:             `max_brief_chars=${options.maxBriefChars}`,
409:             `max_cache_entries=${options.maxCacheEntries}`,
410:             `runtime_ready=${state.runtimeReady}`,
411:             `node_binary=${statusSafeBinary(options.nodeBinary)}`,
412:             `bridge_timeout_ms=${options.bridgeTimeoutMs}`,
413:             `cli_timeout_ms=${options.cliTimeoutMs}`,
414:             `bridge_path=[spec-memory-bridge]`,
415:             `last_bridge_status=${state.lastBridgeStatus}`,
416:             `last_error_code=${state.lastErrorCode ?? 'none'}`,
417:             `last_duration_ms=${state.lastDurationMs}`,
418:             `bridge_invocations=${state.bridgeInvocations}`,
419:             `continuity_lookups=${state.continuityLookups}`,
420:             `cache_entries=${state.continuityCache.size}`,
421:             `cache_hits=${state.cacheHits}`,
422:             `cache_misses=${state.cacheMisses}`,
423:             `cache_hit_rate=${cacheHitRate()}`,
424:             `warm_status=${bridgeStatus.status}`,
425:             `warm_error=${bridgeStatus.error ?? 'none'}`,
426:             `warm_route=${bridgeStatus.metadata?.route ?? 'unknown'}`,
427:             `warm_retryable=${bridgeStatus.metadata?.retryable ?? false}`,
428:             `warm_exit_code=${bridgeStatus.metadata?.exitCode ?? 'none'}`,
429:           ].join('\n');
430:         },
431:       }),
432:     },

147:     maxBriefChars: normalizePositiveInt(options.maxBriefChars, normalizePositiveInt(envMaxBriefChars, DEFAULT_MAX_BRIEF_CHARS)),

167:   if (!value || typeof value !== 'object') {
168:     return JSON.stringify(String(value));

171:   if (Array.isArray(value)) {

225: function extractPrompt(input) {
226:   if (typeof input === 'string') {
227:     return input;
228:   }
229:   if (!input || typeof input !== 'object') {
230:     return null;
231:   }

233:   const candidates = [
234:     input.prompt,
235:     input.text,
236:     input.userPrompt,
237:     input.message,
238:     input.input,
239:     input.properties?.prompt,
240:     input.properties?.message,

245:   for (const candidate of candidates) {

249:     if (candidate && typeof candidate === 'object') {
250:       const content = candidate.content ?? candidate.text;
251:       if (typeof content === 'string' && content.trim()) {

253:       }

255:   }

257:   return null;

263:   }

268:   if (!stdout.trim()) {
269:     return {
270:       brief: null,
271:       status: 'fail_open',
272:       error: 'EMPTY_STDOUT',
273:       metadata: {},
274:     };
275:   }
276: 
277:   try {
278:     const parsed = JSON.parse(stdout.trim());
279:     const status = ['ok', 'skipped', 'degraded', 'fail_open'].includes(parsed?.status)
280:       ? parsed.status
281:       : 'fail_open';
282:     return {
283:       brief: typeof parsed?.brief === 'string' && parsed.brief.trim() ? parsed.brief : null,
284:       status,
285:       metadata: parsed?.metadata && typeof parsed.metadata === 'object' ? parsed.metadata : {},
286:       ...(typeof parsed?.error === 'string' ? { error: promptSafeErrorCode(parsed.error) } : {}),
287:     };
288:   } catch {
289:     return {
290:       brief: null,
291:       status: 'fail_open',
292:       error: 'PARSE_FAIL',
293:       metadata: {},
294:     };
295:   }
296: }
297: 
298: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
299: // 4. SIZE-CAP HELPERS
300: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
301: 
302: /**
303:  * Clamp prompt text to the requested UTF-8 byte budget.
304:  *
305:  * @param {string} prompt - Prompt text to clamp
306:  * @param {number} maxBytes - Maximum UTF-8 byte count to retain
307:  * @returns {string} Original prompt or byte-safe prefix within the budget
308:  */
309: function clampPrompt(prompt, maxBytes) {
310:   if (typeof prompt !== 'string' || Buffer.byteLength(prompt, 'utf8') <= maxBytes) {
311:     return prompt;
312:   }
313: 
314:   let low = 0;
315:   let high = prompt.length;
316:   while (low < high) {
317:     const mid = Math.ceil((low + high) / 2);
318:     if (Buffer.byteLength(prompt.slice(0, mid), 'utf8') <= maxBytes) {
319:       low = mid;
320:       continue;
321:     }
322:     high = mid - 1;
323:   }
324:   return prompt.slice(0, low);
325: }
326: 
327: /**
328:  * Clamp advisor brief text by JavaScript character count.
329:  *
330:  * @param {string} brief - Advisor brief text to clamp
331:  * @param {number} maxChars - Maximum character count to retain
332:  * @returns {string} Original brief or truncated prefix within the limit
333:  */
334: function clampBrief(brief, maxChars) {
335:   if (typeof brief !== 'string' || brief.length <= maxChars) {
336:     return brief;
337:   }
338:   return brief.slice(0, maxChars);
339: }
340: 
341: /**
342:  * Insert a cache value and evict oldest entries by insertion order.
343:  *
344:  * @param {Map<string, Object>} cache - Cache map using insertion order as LRU state
345:  * @param {string} key - Cache key to insert or refresh
346:  * @param {Object} value - Cache value to store
347:  * @param {number} maxEntries - Maximum entries retained after insertion
348:  * @returns {void}
349:  */
350: function insertWithEviction(cache, key, value, maxEntries) {
351:   cache.set(key, value);
352:   while (cache.size > maxEntries) {
353:     const oldest = cache.keys().next().value;
354:     cache.delete(oldest);
355:   }
356: }
357: 
358: function bridgePayloadJson({ prompt, projectDir, options }) {
359:   const basePayload = {
360:     prompt: '',
361:     workspaceRoot: projectDir,
362:     runtime: 'codex',
363:     maxTokens: options.maxTokens,
364:     thresholdConfidence: options.thresholdConfidence,
365:   };
366:   const payloadOverheadBytes = Buffer.byteLength(JSON.stringify(basePayload), 'utf8');
367:   const promptBudgetBytes = Math.max(0, options.maxPromptBytes - payloadOverheadBytes);
368:   return JSON.stringify({
369:     ...basePayload,
370:     prompt: clampPrompt(prompt, promptBudgetBytes),
371:   });
372: }
373: 
374: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
375: // 5. EVENT HELPERS
376: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
377: 
378: function eventPayloadFrom(event) {
379:   if (event?.payload && typeof event.payload === 'object') {
380:     return event.payload;
381:   }
382:   return event;
383: }
384: 
385: function eventTypeFrom(event) {
386:   const payload = eventPayloadFrom(event);
387:   return typeof payload?.type === 'string' ? payload.type : null;
388: }
389: 
390: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
391: // 6. PLUGIN FACTORY
392: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
393: 
394: /**
395:  * Create the Spec Kit skill advisor OpenCode plugin hooks.
396:  *
397:  * @param {import('@opencode-ai/plugin').PluginInput} ctx - OpenCode plugin context
398:  * @param {Object} [rawOptions] - Plugin options
399:  * @param {boolean} [rawOptions.enabled] - Whether the plugin should inject advisor briefs
400:  * @param {number} [rawOptions.cacheTTLMs] - Advisor cache TTL in milliseconds
401:  * @param {number} [rawOptions.cacheTtlMs] - Advisor cache TTL alias in milliseconds
402:  * @param {number} [rawOptions.thresholdConfidence] - Minimum advisor confidence threshold
403:  * @param {number} [rawOptions.maxTokens] - Maximum advisor brief tokens requested from bridge
404:  * @param {string} [rawOptions.nodeBinaryOverride] - Node binary used for the bridge subprocess
405:  * @param {number} [rawOptions.bridgeTimeoutMs] - Bridge subprocess timeout in milliseconds
406:  * @param {number} [rawOptions.maxPromptBytes] - Maximum bridge prompt payload bytes
407:  * @param {number} [rawOptions.maxBriefChars] - Maximum injected advisor brief characters
408:  * @param {number} [rawOptions.maxCacheEntries] - Maximum advisor cache entries
409:  * @param {string} [rawOptions.sourceSignatureOverride] - Test override for advisor source signature
410:  * @returns {Promise<Object>} Hooks with `event`, `experimental.chat.system.transform`, and `tool`
411:  */
412: export default async function MkSkillAdvisorPlugin(ctx, rawOptions) {
413:   const fileConfig = await configPromise;
414:   const merged = { ...fileConfig, ...rawOptions };
415:   const options = normalizeOptions(merged);
416:   const projectDir = normalizeWorkspaceRoot(ctx?.directory);
417: 
418:   // Per-instance state so two plugin instances loaded in the same process maintain independent caches/metrics.
419:   const state = {
420:     advisorCache: new Map(),
421:     // in-flight promise dedup — concurrent identical-key requests share one bridge spawn
422:     inFlight: new Map(),
423:     runtimeReady: false,
424:     lastBridgeStatus: 'uninitialized',
425:     lastErrorCode: null,
426:     lastDurationMs: 0,
427:     bridgeInvocations: 0,
428:     cacheHits: 0,
429:     cacheMisses: 0,
430:     advisorLookups: 0,
431:     disabledReason: !options.enabled
432:       ? (disabledEnvName() ?? 'config_enabled_false')
433:       : null,
434:   };
435: 
436:   /**
437:    * Run the skill-advisor bridge subprocess and parse its JSON response.
438:    * The bridge receives stdin JSON from `bridgePayloadJson`, returns stdout JSON
439:    * parsed by `parseBridgeResponse`, gets SIGTERM on timeout, and gets SIGKILL
440:    * after one additional second if the process has not settled.
441:    *
442:    * @param {Object} params - Bridge invocation parameters
443:    * @param {string} params.projectDir - Working directory for the bridge subprocess
444:    * @param {string} params.prompt - Prompt sent to the bridge over stdin JSON
445:    * @param {Object} params.options - Normalized plugin options
446:    * @param {string} params.options.nodeBinary - Node binary used to spawn the bridge
447:    * @param {number} params.options.bridgeTimeoutMs - Timeout before SIGTERM is sent
448:    * @returns {Promise<Object>} Parsed stdout JSON with `brief`, `status`, `error`, and `metadata`
449:    */
450:   function runBridge({ projectDir, prompt, options }) {
451:     const startedAt = Date.now();
452:     state.bridgeInvocations += 1;
453: 
454:     return new Promise((resolve) => {
455:       const child = spawn(options.nodeBinary, [BRIDGE_PATH], {
456:         cwd: projectDir,
457:         env: process.env,
458:         stdio: ['pipe', 'pipe', 'pipe'],
459:       });
460: 
461:       let stdout = '';
462:       let settled = false;
463:       let timedOut = false;
464:       let sigkillTimer = null;
465:       const timeout = setTimeout(() => {
466:         if (settled) {
467:           return;
468:         }
469:         timedOut = true;
470:         child.kill('SIGTERM');
471:         sigkillTimer = setTimeout(() => {
472:           if (!settled) {
473:             child.kill('SIGKILL');
474:           }
475:         }, 1000);
476:       }, options.bridgeTimeoutMs);
477: 
478:       child.stdout?.setEncoding?.('utf8');
479:       child.stdout?.on('data', (chunk) => {
480:         stdout += String(chunk);
481:       });
482: 
483:       child.on('error', () => {
484:         if (settled) {
485:           return;
486:         }
487:         settled = true;
488:         clearTimeout(timeout);
489:         if (sigkillTimer) {
490:           clearTimeout(sigkillTimer);
491:         }
492:         state.lastBridgeStatus = 'fail_open';
493:         state.lastErrorCode = timedOut ? 'TIMEOUT' : 'SPAWN_ERROR';
494:         state.lastDurationMs = Date.now() - startedAt;
495:         resolve({
496:           brief: null,
497:           status: 'fail_open',
498:           error: state.lastErrorCode,
499:           metadata: {},
500:         });
501:       });
502: 
503:       child.on('close', (code) => {
504:         if (settled) {
505:           return;
506:         }
507:         settled = true;
508:         clearTimeout(timeout);
509:         if (sigkillTimer) {
510:           clearTimeout(sigkillTimer);
511:         }
512: 
513:         const response = timedOut
514:           ? { brief: null, status: 'fail_open', error: 'TIMEOUT', metadata: {} }
515:           : parseBridgeResponse(stdout);
516:         if (!timedOut && code !== 0 && response.status !== 'fail_open') {
517:           response.status = 'fail_open';
518:           response.brief = null;
519:           response.error = 'NONZERO_EXIT';
520:         }
521: 
522:         state.lastBridgeStatus = response.status;
523:         state.lastErrorCode = response.error ?? null;
524:         state.lastDurationMs = Date.now() - startedAt;
525:         resolve(response);
526:       });
527: 
528:       child.stdin?.end(bridgePayloadJson({ prompt, projectDir, options }));
529:     });
530:   }
531: 
532:   /**
533:    * Resolve advisor context for a prompt using cache and in-flight deduplication.
534:    *
535:    * @param {Object} params - Advisor lookup parameters
536:    * @param {string} params.projectDir - Workspace root used by the advisor bridge
537:    * @param {string} params.prompt - User prompt text for advisor matching
538:    * @param {string} params.sessionID - Session identifier included in the cache key
539:    * @param {Object} params.options - Normalized plugin options
540:    * @returns {Promise<Object>} Advisor response with optional `brief` and status metadata
541:    */
542:   async function getAdvisorContext({ projectDir, prompt, sessionID, options }) {
543:     const sourceSignature = options.sourceSignatureOverride ?? advisorSourceSignature();
544:     const workspaceRoot = normalizeWorkspaceRoot(projectDir);
545:     const key = cacheKeyForPrompt(prompt, options, sessionID, sourceSignature, workspaceRoot);
546:     const now = Date.now();
547:     const cached = state.advisorCache.get(key);
548: 
549:     state.advisorLookups += 1;
550:     if (cached && cached.expiresAt > now) {
551:       state.advisorCache.delete(key);
552:       state.advisorCache.set(key, cached);
553:       state.cacheHits += 1;
554:       return cached.response;
555:     }
556: 
557:     const inFlight = state.inFlight.get(key);
558:     if (inFlight) {
559:       state.cacheHits += 1;
560:       return inFlight;
561:     }
562: 
563:     state.cacheMisses += 1;
564:     const promise = runBridge({ projectDir, prompt, options })
565:       .finally(() => {
566:         state.inFlight.delete(key);
567:       });
568:     state.inFlight.set(key, promise);
569:     const response = await promise;
570:     if (response.status === 'ok' && response.brief) {
571:       insertWithEviction(state.advisorCache, key, {
572:         response,
573:         expiresAt: now + options.cacheTTLMs,
574:         updatedAt: new Date(now).toISOString(),
575:       }, options.maxCacheEntries);
576:     } else {
577:       state.advisorCache.delete(key);
578:     }
579:     return response;
580:   }
581: 
582:   function resetRuntimeState() {
583:     state.advisorCache.clear();
584:     state.runtimeReady = false;
585:     state.lastBridgeStatus = 'uninitialized';
586:     state.lastErrorCode = null;
587:     state.lastDurationMs = 0;
588:     state.bridgeInvocations = 0;
589:     state.cacheHits = 0;
590:     state.cacheMisses = 0;
591:     state.advisorLookups = 0;
592:     state.disabledReason = !options.enabled
593:       ? (disabledEnvName() ?? 'config_enabled_false')
594:       : null;
595:   }
596: 
597:   function cacheHitRate() {
598:     const total = state.cacheHits + state.cacheMisses;
599:     return total === 0 ? 0 : Number((state.cacheHits / total).toFixed(3));
600:   }
601: 
602:   /**
603:    * Append an advisor brief to the OpenCode chat system output.
604:    *
605:    * @param {Object} [input={}] - OpenCode system-transform hook input
606:    * @param {Object} [output={ system: [] }] - OpenCode system-transform hook output
607:    * @param {Array<string>} [output.system] - System context entries mutated in place
608:    * @returns {Promise<void>} Resolves after optional advisor context injection
609:    */
610:   async function appendAdvisorBrief(input = {}, output = { system: [] }) {
611:     if (!output || typeof output !== 'object') {
612:       return;
613:     }
614:     output.system = Array.isArray(output?.system) ? output.system : [];
615: 
616:     if (!options.enabled) {
617:       return;
618:     }
619:     state.runtimeReady = true;
620: 
621:     let prompt = extractPrompt(input);
622:     const sessionID = sessionIdFrom(input);
623:     if (!prompt && sessionID !== '__global__') {
624:       try {
625:         const result = await ctx?.client?.session?.messages?.({
626:           path: { id: sessionID },
627:           query: { directory: projectDir, limit: 20 },
628:         });
629:         const messages = Array.isArray(result?.data)
630:           ? result.data
631:           : (Array.isArray(result) ? result : []);
632: 
633:         for (const entry of [...messages].reverse()) {
634:           const info = entry?.info ?? entry?.message ?? entry;
635:           if (info?.role !== 'user') {
636:             continue;
637:           }
638: 
639:           const parts = Array.isArray(entry?.parts)
640:             ? entry.parts
641:             : (Array.isArray(info?.parts) ? info.parts : []);
642:           const textPart = [...parts]
643:             .reverse()
644:             .find((part) => part?.type === 'text' && typeof part.text === 'string' && part.text.trim());
645:           if (textPart) {
646:             prompt = textPart.text;
647:             break;
648:           }
649: 
650:           prompt = extractPrompt(info);
651:           if (prompt) {
652:             break;
653:           }
654:         }
655:       } catch {
656:         prompt = null;
657:       }
658:     }
659: 
660:     if (!prompt) {
661:       state.lastBridgeStatus = 'skipped';
662:       state.lastErrorCode = 'MISSING_PROMPT';
663:       return;
664:     }
665: 
666:     const response = await getAdvisorContext({
667:       projectDir,
668:       prompt,
669:       sessionID,
670:       options,
671:     });
672:     if (response.brief) {
673:       output.system.push(clampBrief(response.brief, options.maxBriefChars));
674:     } else {
675:       output.system.push(HYGIENE_DIRECTIVE);
676:     }
677:   }
678: 
679:   return {
680:     event: async ({ event }) => {
681:       const eventPayload = eventPayloadFrom(event);
682:       const eventType = eventTypeFrom(event);
683: 
684:       if (eventType === 'session.created') {
685:         if (options.enabled) {
686:           state.runtimeReady = true;
687:           state.lastBridgeStatus = 'ready';
688:         }
689:         return;
690:       }
691: 
692:       if (eventType === 'session.deleted') {
693:         const sessionID = normalizeSessionID(eventPayload?.properties?.sessionID
694:           || eventPayload?.properties?.info?.sessionID
695:           || eventPayload?.properties?.info?.id
696:           || sessionIdFrom(eventPayload));
697:         for (const key of [...state.advisorCache.keys()]) {
698:           if (key.startsWith(`${sessionID}::`)) {
699:             state.advisorCache.delete(key);
700:           }
701:         }
702:         return;
703:       }
704: 
705:       if (eventType === 'server.instance.disposed' || eventType === 'global.disposed') {
706:         resetRuntimeState();
707:       }
708:     },
709: 
710:     'experimental.chat.system.transform': appendAdvisorBrief,
711: 
712:     tool: {
713:       spec_kit_skill_advisor_status: tool({
714:         description: 'Show Spec Kit skill advisor plugin prompt-safe cache status',
715:         args: {},
716:         async execute() {
717:           return [
718:             `plugin_id=${PLUGIN_ID}`,
719:             `enabled=${options.enabled}`,
720:             `disabled_reason=${state.disabledReason ?? 'none'}`,
721:             `cache_ttl_ms=${options.cacheTTLMs}`,
722:             `threshold_confidence=${options.thresholdConfidence}`,
723:             `max_tokens=${options.maxTokens}`,
724:             `max_prompt_bytes=${options.maxPromptBytes}`,
725:             `max_brief_chars=${options.maxBriefChars}`,
726:             `max_cache_entries=${options.maxCacheEntries}`,
727:             `runtime_ready=${state.runtimeReady}`,
728:             `node_binary=${statusSafeBinary(options.nodeBinary)}`,
729:             `bridge_timeout_ms=${options.bridgeTimeoutMs}`,
730:             `bridge_path=${statusSafePath('skill-advisor-bridge')}`,
731:             `last_bridge_status=${state.lastBridgeStatus}`,
732:             `last_runtime_status=${state.lastBridgeStatus}`,
733:             `last_error_code=${state.lastErrorCode ?? 'none'}`,
734:             `last_runtime_error=${state.lastErrorCode ?? 'none'}`,
735:             `last_duration_ms=${state.lastDurationMs}`,
736:             `bridge_invocations=${state.bridgeInvocations}`,
737:             `advisor_lookups=${state.advisorLookups}`,
738:             `cache_entries=${state.advisorCache.size}`,
739:             `cache_hits=${state.cacheHits}`,
740:             `cache_misses=${state.cacheMisses}`,
741:             `cache_hit_rate=${cacheHitRate()}`,
742:           ].join('\n');
743:         },
744:       }),
745:     },
746:   };
747: }
